title: AVEL GORDLY CENTER FOR HEALING - 211info
text: AVEL GORDLY CENTER FOR HEALING AVEL GORDLY CENTER FOR HEALING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally responsive mental health services, including African American–focused services. Offers individual, group, couples, and family counseling, as well as medication support. Helps adults, couples, and families address concerns such as depression, anxiety, trauma, major life changes, substance use, DUI treatment, problem gambling, and ongoing health conditions. Works to reduce stigma around mental health and encourage healing. 621 SW Alder Street Suite 520 Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted. Sliding scale fees for mental health services are available to uninsured Multnomah County residents who identify as African or African American and have incomes at or below 200% of the federal poverty level. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-6pm, Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 418-5311 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 418-5311 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call to schedule an appointment FEES: Accepts OHP, private insurance, and self-pay. Sliding scale fees may be available for alcohol and drug addiction treatment, particularly for African American communities in Multnomah County. Gambling treatment is offered free of charge to all. DOCUMENTS: Insurance information, if applicable. Proof of income and address if applying for sliding scale fees. AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Community Mental Health Agencies Community Mental Health Agencies * African American Community Gambling Disorder Counseling If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
